contextBlurb: Washington regulates stablecoins through its Uniform Money Services Act (UMSA, RCW 19.230), which requires money transmission licenses for virtual currency businesses. No dedicated stablecoin legislation exists; regulation is applied via the existing money transmission framework.

legislation.title: Uniform Money Services Act (UMSA)
legislation.summary: Washington's money transmission law, interpreted to cover virtual currency and stablecoin businesses. Requires licensing from the Washington State Department of Financial Institutions for entities transmitting digital assets, including stablecoin issuers and exchangers. No dedicated stablecoin statute exists.
